activities_projects.title: Analyse the Meaning of Secularism
activities_projects.objective: Develop a deeper understanding of secularism in the Indian context
steps: Write the definition of secularism
Explain how Indian secularism is different from other countries
Give two examples where secularism is practiced in India
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Explains the concept and features of Indian secularism
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of explanation and examples (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Case Study on Religious Conflict
activities_projects.objective: Understand how secularism helps resolve conflicts
steps: Describe a situation where two religious groups have a conflict
Explain how the government should respond
Suggest solutions based on secular principles
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies secular principles to real-life situations
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of analysis and solutions (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Fundamental Rights Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Understand constitutional protection of religious freedom
steps: Write about the Fundamental Right to freedom of religion
Explain its importance in a secular democracy
Give an example where this right protects citizens
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands constitutional rights and their role

activities_projects.title: Debate: Should the State Intervene in Religion?
activities_projects.objective: Develop critical thinking about the role of the state
steps: Prepare arguments supporting state intervention
Prepare arguments opposing state intervention
Present your opinion with reasons

activities_projects.title: Research Religious Discrimination
activities_projects.objective: Understand the problems caused by religious discrimination
steps: Find one news report related to religious discrimination
Write a summary of the event
Explain how secular laws could prevent such issues
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Connects current events with secular principles
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of research and explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Compare Secularism in Two Countries
steps: Choose India and another country
Write how religion and government are related in both countries
List two similarities and two differences

activities_projects.title: Role of the Constitution in Secularism
activities_projects.objective: Understand how the Constitution supports secular values
steps: Write two constitutional principles supporting secularism
Explain how they protect religious freedom
Give one example for each principle

activities_projects.title: Poster: Secular India
activities_projects.objective: Express the idea of secularism creatively
steps: Design a poster showing people of different religions living together
Add slogans promoting secularism
Explain the message of the poster

activities_projects.title: Essay on Unity in Diversity
steps: Write an essay on unity in diversity
Explain the role of secularism in maintaining harmony
Include examples from Indian society
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity and depth of essay (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Critical Thinking: Secularism in Daily Life
activities_projects.objective: Apply the concept of secularism to everyday situations
steps: Write three situations from daily life where secular values are important
Explain how people should behave in each situation
Relate them to the idea of equality and respect
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies secular values to real-life contexts
